The Anniversary Raid in Morimens is pure unrelenting horror. I’ve seen less trauma in a Kharkiv basement chat group.
I tried making it hard once: turned my phone off, wrapped it in a vyshyvanka, and hid it in a jar of pickled tomatoes. Stage still cleared. My Awakeners apparently called in a Bayraktar strike on the deploy button, then auto-cleared day 4 while arguing over who gets the last limited pull.
Tohuwabohu spawned, looked at my team, whispered “блять” and voluntarily walked into a sunflower field to rethink it's life choices. Tohuwabohu saw the damage numbers and immediately filed for political asylum in Lviv.
The devs set the difficulty by asking a sleepy babushka with a sunflower in her hair to balance it. She patted the server on the head and smiled, translation: free silver and pulls for everyone.
My cat stepped on the screen once. Cleared the the gnosis bosses. Now he demands canned tuna and threatens to mobilize if I don’t deliver.
This isn’t endgame content, it’s humanitarian aid with a side of embarrassment.

Gmurphy's Exalt inflicts 15 stacks of Birth Ritual at max skill level. Gmurphy's Posse inflicts 10 stacks of Birth Ritual at E3 when you switching to Raging Waves.

Raging Waves is a tentacle stance. Click on the yellow circle to switch tentacle stances. Raging Waves stance, or red stance, make tentacles attack each time you deal an instance of damage. Red stance removes a tentacle at the end of the turn, and so having +1 tentacle is a prerequisite of switching to it.

Well, you've got all the best wheels, so that's solid. My suggestions right now would be, based on what you have - if you like Caro, Thais E2 and Gelot E3 are extremely powerful investments and can clear all content. If you like Blue, +4 GMurphy can carry you through endgame, but is a steep investment given how many options you have. If you like Purple, Castor is a great long-term investment, as is Clem. If you want Chaos, Kathi at E3 is among the best units in the game. GDoll at +4 is also capable of carrying a team by herself in most DTide iterations. These are options for Prototype investment. 

Because you have so many options however, I'd wait until you've exhausted your Luminous Core supply (or a hit a point where you don't want to pull anymore) before making any decisions about where to put your prototypes. Get through the end of the story (finish Arc 2 Chapter 4), and then consider where you want your resources to go.
